LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER CAROL ROGERS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING RELIEF FROM THE ORDER SUSTAINING THE DEMURRER AS TO PLAINTIFF CAROL ROGERS' FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED ON THE CONDITION THAT WITHIN 30 DAYS MS. ROGERS OR HER COUNSEL PAYS $1000.00 TO DEFENDANTS FOR REASONABLE COMPENSATORY LEGAL FEES. IF THE CONDITION IS SATISFIED, THE NOVEMBER 23, 2015 DEMURRER ORDER WILL BE VACATED AND A HEARING WILL BE SET ON AUGUST 25, 2016 ON DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE DATES FOR THE FILING OF MS. ROGERS' OPPOSITION TO THE DEMURRER AND ANY REPLY PAPERS ARE DETERMINED PER CCP 1005 ARE TRIGGERED OFF THE AUGUST 25, 2016 HEARING DATE. MR. REED IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976.

DISCOVERY 302, DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANTS RUTH MACCHIARINI, JEANNE GALE, ESQ, AND GALE & NIELSEN'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER AND FOR SANCTIONS IS CONTINUED TO DECEMBER 27, 2016, AT 9:00 AM TO GIVE MOVING PARTY THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.7(B). MOVING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS TO JUDGE PRO TEM, B. DOUGLAS ROBBINS AT 1 POST STREET SUITE 800, SAN FRANCISCO, CA 94402, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN NOVEMBER 29, 2016, AT 4:00 PM OR THE MOTION WILL BE DENIED. THIS COVER LETTER SHALL ALSO BE SERVED ON ALL PARTIES. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT 3.1110(F). JUDGE PRO TEM: DOUGLAS ROBBINS; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; NOT REPORTED. (D302/JPT)

ORDER GRANTING MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS JEANNE GALE AND GALE & NIELSEN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION, IS GRANTED AND PLAINTIFF CAROL ROGERS' CROSS-MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. MS. GALE AND HER LAW FIRM SATISFIED THEIR INITIAL SUMMARY JUDGMENT BURDEN THAT THEY ARE NOT LIABLE UNDER THE PROMISSORY NOTE AND MS. ROGERS HAS NOT PRESENTED ANY EVIDENCE CREATING A TRIABLE DISPUTE. NONE OF THE EVIDENCE CITED IN MS. ROGERS' SEPARATE STATEMENTS SHOWS THAT MS. GALE OR GALE & NIELSON ARE OBLIGORS ON THE PROMISSORY NOTE OR TOOK OR FAILED TO TAKE ANY ACTIONS THAT WARRANT IMPOSITION OF LIABILITY AGAINST THEM ON THE NOTE. APART FROM LACKING ANY MERIT, MS. ROGERS' CROSS-MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS PROCEDURALLY DEFICIENT BECAUSE IT DID NOT COMPLY WITH THE REQUIRED 75 DAY NOTICE MANDATED BY CCP 437C(A)(2). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976; DEPT. 302

calendar.matters:
COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Feb-07-2017 Text Ruling

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Feb-06-2017 Assigned To Trial Court

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar Feb-06-2017 Continued To Feb-07-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 611

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jan-04-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ARTHUR G. NIELSEN OF THE GALE & NIELSEN LAW OFFICE, TEL. NO. 1-707-269-0167 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendants Jeanne Gale and Gale & Nielsen's motion for summary judgment is granted and plaintiff Carol Rogers' cross-motion for summary judgment is denied. Ms. Gale and her law firm satisfied their initial summary judgment burden that they are not liable under the promissory note and Ms. Rogers has not presented any evidence creating a triable dispute. None of the evidence cited in Ms. Rogers' separate statements shows that Ms. Gale or Gale & Nielson are obligors on the promissory note or took or failed to take any actions that warrant imposition of liability against them on the note. Apart from lacking any merit, Ms. Rogers' cross-motion for summary judgment is procedurally deficient because it did not comply with the required 75 day notice mandated by CCP 437c(a)(2). (Cuff v. Grossmont Union High School Dist. (2013) 221 Cal.App.4th 582, 595-596.)
Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/hk

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTS RUTH MACCHIARINI, JEANNE GALE, ESQ, and GALE & NIELSEN'S Motion For Protective Order And For Sanctions is continued to December 27, 2016, at 9:00 AM to give Moving Party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.7(B). Moving Party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Judge Pro Tem, B. Douglas Robbins at 1 Post Street Suite 800, San Francisco, CA 94402, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than November 29, 2016, at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. This cover letter shall also be served on all parties. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per California Rules of Court 3.1110(f).

Judge Pro Tem: Douglas Robbins; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302/jpt)

Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Aug-25-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, John Reed, Esq. (415-251-1224) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Ryan Hurley of Gale & Nielsen (707-269-0167) appears as counsel for defendants/demurring parties

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

Defendants Ruth Macchiarini and Jeanne Gale's Demurrer To First Amended Complaint of Carol Rogers is OVERRULED. The first amended complaint adequately alleges that Ms. Macchiarini, individually and as trustee of the Doris Pisani Trust, is liable on the promissory note. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Laura Reding, CSR 711, email: lreding@sftc.org. (D302)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-07-2016 Continued To Oct-12-16 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion For Order Granting Relief On Demurrer As To Plaintiff Carol Rogers First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-23-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Attorney John Reed telephone number 415-251-1224 for Plaintiff Carol Rogers.
Ryan Hurley of Gale & Nielsen telephone number 707-269-0167 for all Defendants.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. PETITIONER CAROL ROGERS' Motion For Order Granting Relief From The Order Sustaining The Demurrer As To Plaintiff Carol Rogers' First Amended Complaint is granted on the condition that within 30 days Ms. Rogers or her counsel pays $1000.00 to defendants for reasonable compensatory legal fees. If the condition is satisfied, the November 23, 2015 demurrer order will be vacated and a hearing will be set on August 25, 2016 on defendants' demurrer to the first amended complaint. The dates for the filing of Ms. Rogers' opposition to the demurrer and any reply papers are determined per CCP 1005 are triggered off the August 25, 2016 hearing date. Mr. Reed is to prepare a form of order and comply with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)
